title: The Photographer's Eye
excerpt: A definitive guide to photographic composition, teaching the visual principles that distinguish compelling photographs from snapshots.
keyIdeas: Composition is the foundation of photography — it transforms seeing into expression
The frame is the fundamental act of photography — choosing what to include and exclude
Design principles (balance, rhythm, contrast, proportion) apply to photography as to any visual art
Light, color, and moment are uniquely photographic tools of composition
Breaking the rules of composition requires understanding them first
Post-processing is part of composition, not a separate activity
keyTakeaways: Learn the rules of composition thoroughly before deciding which to break
The frame is your most powerful tool — every edge is an opportunity
Move your feet before zooming — physical position changes perspective fundamentally
Understand how the viewer's eye moves through the image
Pre-visualize the final image before pressing the shutter
whoShouldRead: Beginner photographers who want to move beyond snapshots
Experienced photographers seeking to deepen their compositional understanding
Digital photographers who want to integrate composition with post-processing
Designers who work with photographic images
Anyone who wants to understand why some photographs work and others don't
whoShouldSkip: Photographers interested only in technical camera operation
Advanced professionals who have already mastered composition
Those seeking a philosophical or critical analysis of photography
Readers who prefer a lightweight, inspirational approach over systematic instruction
